question: A meander develops when
options: Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill
The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend

question: The concept of 'watershed' refers to
options: Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas

question: 'Attrition' as a river process
options: One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill

question: A 'stack' is a coastal feature formed when
options: The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Particles bounce along the river bed

question: The concept of 'urban heat island'
options: The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas

question: 'Corrasion' (abrasion) in rivers
options: A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
Particles bounce along the river bed
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material

question: A 'raised beach' indicates
options: Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill
Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)

question: The concept of 'urban sprawl'
options: The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas

options: Particles bounce along the river bed
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity

options: The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend

options: Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population

options: One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
